input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. Let me provide guidance based on my knowledge.

In .NET 9 and earlier, MAUI used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` to handle keyboard appearance, but this API was deprecated in Android 30+ and was removed/changed in .NET 10.

The modern approach in .NET 10 (and Android) is to use `WindowInsets` / edge-to-edge display with proper inset handling. Specifically, the `Resize` mode was deprecated in favor of using `WindowInsetsCompat` and handling the IME (input method editor) insets.


For .NET 10, I should look at using MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature or handling it through platform-specific Android code with `WindowInsetsCompat`. The core issue is that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated in Android API 30, and .NET 10 moved to edge-to-edge rendering by default, so the solution now involves responding to IME inset changes rather than relying on the old resize adjustment mode. Setting up the window insets listener... I'm weighing the practical options here. The manifest approach with `adjustResize` is straightforward but might not handle edge-to-edge layouts well. MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` is convenient for general cases, but for a chat interface where the entire input bar needs to move up with the keyboard, manually listening to IME insets gives the most control. That's probably the best path forward for this specific use case.
